title: Brachyactis ciliata
intro: Brachyactis ciliata, also known as Himalayan knotgrass, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and exhibiting antibacterial and antifungal activities. The bioactive constituents of Brachyactis ciliata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Brachyactis ciliata is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and gastrointestinal disorders.
